_about: No-assumptions sweep across every tunable VocoderParams field. Each knob has 2 values bracketing its documented useful range. 2^13 = 8192 combos total. Use rank_sweep.py to find the top performers objectively; then blind-A/B the top ~10 to confirm.
_runtime: imbe_tune decodes at roughly 50x realtime per combo on modern hardware. For a 10-second .imbe input: ~0.2 s per combo single-pass, ~0.5 s per combo with --multipass. Full 8192-combo sweep = ~25-30 minutes single-pass, ~60-90 minutes with --multipass. Across N source files, multiply by N (rank_sweep.py averages metrics across same-combo across sources).
_disk: Each combo produces a .wav + .json sidecar. For a 10-second input, ~16 KB per .wav + ~0.5 KB sidecar -> ~135 MB total. Per source. Plan accordingly if sweeping many sources.
_workflow:
1) ./build/imbe_tune --multipass --input-dir /tmp/imbe --sweep utils/sweep-all.json --output-dir /tmp/sw_all
2) python3 utils/rank_sweep.py --top 20 --csv /tmp/sw_all/ranking.csv /tmp/sw_all/*
3) Look at the top 10 in the CSV - which knob values keep appearing? Those are the high-impact knobs for your audio. The ones that scatter randomly across the top 10 are not high-impact (or are in their good range already).
4) Build a focused 20-30-combo sweep on the 2-4 high-impact knobs with 3-4 values each. Use that for blind_compare.py.
_endpoints_chosen_to_be: Wide enough that each knob's effect is fully exercised, narrow enough to avoid wasting combos on values you'd never ship. See the inline comments in software_imbe_decoder.h for what each pair brackets.
